core_model.structure_summary: A probability distribution specified model-independently through its cumulants, expanded (Edgeworth/Gram-Charlier style) about a Gaussian via Hermite polynomials, from which a small-parameter asymptotic quadratic expansion of an implied-transform functional and power-law scalings of its slope and curvature are derived.

variables.meaning: standardized log-return random variable whose distribution is expanded
small asymptotic parameter (time to maturity) driving the power-law expansion
third cumulant / skewness controlling first-order slope term
fourth cumulant / kurtosis controlling curvature term
scaling exponents (including a Hurst-type exponent) governing power-law rates as the small parameter vanishes

notes: Poor fit to the dynamical controlled vocabulary. The genuine core is a distribution-based, cumulant/Hermite (Edgeworth-type) asymptotic expansion of the log-return law and the induced implied-volatility expansion, so I classified it 'statistical_model'. scale_invariance captures the power-law small-time asymptotics and the rough-volatility Hurst exponent; multiplicative_noise reflects the underlying stochastic-volatility representation dZ=-1/2 v dt + sqrt(v) dB the framework subsumes, though the results are explicitly model-independent. Only two vocabulary tags genuinely apply; did not pad to reach 3-8.
